clean_output:
article_id: art-2026-03-04-018
summary: The article supports a bounded claim that a coordinated context-engineering workflow may improve multi-agent code-assistant performance, but the mechanism and evidence remain only lightly specified in the provided material.
review_confidence: medium
mechanisms:
  - claim_text: A coordinated context engineering workflow can improve accuracy and reliability for multi-agent LLM code assistants on real-world codebases.
    mechanism_text: The draft attributes the improvement to a structured workflow that coordinates multiple tools and agents to supply context more effectively during coding tasks.
    bounds: The provided material does not specify the internal causal steps, task routing logic, or quantitative contribution of each tool, so the mechanism is only supported at a high level.
    confidence: medium
bounded_claims:
  - claim_text: The system outperforms existing frameworks on accuracy and reliability.
    bounds: The provided context says performance was compared against existing frameworks, but it does not include benchmark design, metrics, effect sizes, or replication details.
  - claim_text: The workflow demonstrates stronger performance on real-world codebases.
    bounds: This claim is presented in the findings and draft, but the excerpt does not provide dataset scope, task composition, or failure cases, so the result should be treated as bounded rather than fully established.
  - claim_text: The paper establishes why the workflow works beyond reporting better outcomes.
